title: The Synaptic Cleft
script_summary: Explore the fascinating world of the synaptic cleft where neurons communicate.

segments.voiceover: Neurons don’t quite touch — they send signals across a tiny gap.
This is the synaptic cleft. Just 20 nanometers wide, it’s a chemical highway.
When a neuron fires, it releases neurotransmitters into this gap, sending messages.
Research shows this process is crucial for everything from movement to memory.
So, next time you think, remember: your thoughts travel through this tiny gap.
Curious about how your brain works? Hit the follow button for more!
